title: Verify firmware version updates on Home screen immediately after successful DFU without app restart
steps: 1. Launch the app and navigate to the Home screen.
2. Record the current firmware version displayed on the Home screen (e.g., 'Firmware: v1.0.0').
3. Navigate to the device settings page and record the firmware version displayed there.
4. Initiate a DFU (Device Firmware Update) process with a new firmware version (e.g., v2.0.0).
5. Wait for the DFU process to complete successfully (verify via success pop-up/message).
6. Dismiss any success pop-ups.
7. Without restarting the app, navigate back to the Home screen.
8. Observe the firmware version displayed on the Home screen.
9. Navigate to the device settings page and observe the firmware version displayed there.
expected: The firmware version displayed on both the Home screen and the device settings page immediately reflects the new version (e.g., 'Firmware: v2.0.0') without requiring an app restart. The version matches the firmware that was just installed via DFU.

title: Verify firmware version remains updated on Home screen and settings page after app restart following DFU
steps: 1. Launch the app and navigate to the Home screen.
2. Record the current firmware version displayed on the Home screen.
3. Navigate to the device settings page and record the firmware version displayed there.
4. Initiate a DFU process with a new firmware version (e.g., v3.1.0).
5. Wait for the DFU process to complete successfully.
6. Dismiss any success pop-ups.
7. Force close (terminate) the app completely.
8. Relaunch the app.
9. Navigate to the Home screen and observe the firmware version displayed.
10. Navigate to the device settings page and observe the firmware version displayed.
expected: After relaunching the app, the firmware version displayed on both the Home screen and the device settings page shows the new version (e.g., 'Firmware: v3.1.0') that was installed via DFU. The version is consistent across both screens and matches the updated firmware.

title: Verify firmware version consistency between Home screen and settings page before and after DFU
steps: 1. Launch the app and navigate to the Home screen.
2. Capture the firmware version displayed on the Home screen (Version_A_Home).
3. Navigate to the device settings page and capture the firmware version displayed there (Version_A_Settings).
4. Verify that Version_A_Home == Version_A_Settings.
5. Initiate a DFU process to update to a new firmware version (e.g., v4.0.0).
6. Wait for the DFU process to complete successfully.
7. Dismiss any success pop-ups.
8. Without restarting the app, navigate to the Home screen and capture the firmware version (Version_B_Home).
9. Navigate to the device settings page and capture the firmware version (Version_B_Settings).
10. Verify that Version_B_Home == Version_B_Settings.
11. Verify that Version_B_Home != Version_A_Home (i.e., the version has changed).
12. Force close and relaunch the app.
13. Navigate to the Home screen and capture the firmware version (Version_C_Home).
14. Navigate to the device settings page and capture the firmware version (Version_C_Settings).
15. Verify that Version_C_Home == Version_C_Settings.
16. Verify that Version_C_Home == Version_B_Home (i.e., the version persists after restart).
expected: The firmware version is consistent between the Home screen and settings page at all stages: before DFU, immediately after DFU (without restart), and after app restart. The version updates immediately after DFU and persists correctly after app relaunch.

title: Verify firmware version display after DFU when downgrading to an older firmware version
steps: 1. Launch the app and navigate to the Home screen.
2. Record the current firmware version (e.g., v5.0.0).
3. Initiate a DFU process to downgrade to an older firmware version (e.g., v4.2.0).
4. Wait for the DFU process to complete successfully.
5. Dismiss any success pop-ups.
6. Without restarting the app, navigate to the Home screen and observe the firmware version.
7. Navigate to the device settings page and observe the firmware version.
8. Force close and relaunch the app.
9. Navigate to the Home screen and observe the firmware version.
10. Navigate to the device settings page and observe the firmware version.
expected: The firmware version updates immediately to the older version (v4.2.0) on both the Home screen and settings page without requiring an app restart. The version remains correctly displayed after app restart as well.

title: Verify firmware version display after multiple consecutive DFU operations
steps: 1. Launch the app and navigate to the Home screen.
2. Record the initial firmware version (e.g., v1.0.0).
3. Perform a DFU to update to version v2.0.0. Wait for completion.
4. Without restarting, verify the Home screen shows v2.0.0.
5. Immediately perform a second DFU to update to version v3.0.0. Wait for completion.
6. Without restarting, navigate to the Home screen and observe the firmware version.
7. Navigate to the device settings page and observe the firmware version.
8. Force close and relaunch the app.
9. Navigate to the Home screen and observe the firmware version.
10. Navigate to the device settings page and observe the firmware version.
expected: After each consecutive DFU, the firmware version updates immediately on both the Home screen and settings page without requiring an app restart. After the second DFU, both screens display v3.0.0. The version persists correctly after app restart.
